title: podman container forward ports firewalld
body:
how to run a container with a port so that it is only available inside the host
For example,
1. Сontainer  nginx port look outside and look  to backend port, 
2. Container only inside, backend, but nginx sees it.

comments.body: Do you want to bind the port exclusively to localhost on the host system? As written, your question isn't very clear.

> Do you want to bind the port exclusively to localhost on the host system? As written, your question isn't very clear.

Yes. It's a shame that the port is immediately visible on the physical interface. Administrators need to be given a choice. This is a security issue.
you can use `-p 127.0.0.1:8080:80` to make it only bind the locahost ip.
If you just want to make the nginx container talk to the backend container use a network instead of port forwarding.
